Catholic.title: Solemnity of Mary, Mother of God
Catholic.description: Honors Mary’s role in the mystery of Christ’s incarnation.

Catholic.title: Epiphany
Catholic.description: Commemorates the revelation of Jesus Christ to the nations.

Catholic.title: Baptism of the Lord
Catholic.description: Recalls the baptism of Jesus and the beginning of his public ministry.

Catholic.title: Second Sunday in Ordinary Time
Catholic.description: A Sunday in Ordinary Time focusing on the teachings and ministry of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Conversion of Saint Paul
Catholic.description: Commemorates the conversion of Paul the Apostle and the call to Christian unity.

Catholic.title: Third Sunday in Ordinary Time
Catholic.description: A Sunday in Ordinary Time focusing on the teachings and ministry of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Fourth Sunday in Ordinary Time
Catholic.description: A Sunday in Ordinary Time focusing on the teachings and ministry of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Presentation of the Lord
Catholic.description: Commemorates Jesus being presented at the Temple in Jerusalem.

Catholic.title: Sixth Sunday in Ordinary Time
Catholic.description: A Sunday in Ordinary Time focusing on the teachings and ministry of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Lent Begins
Catholic.description: From today Lent begins, a season of repentance, prayer, and renewal.

Catholic.description: Calls believers to repentance and spiritual renewal during Lent.

Catholic.title: Saint Joseph
Catholic.description: Honors Saint Joseph, guardian of the Holy Family.

Catholic.title: Annunciation
Catholic.description: Marks the angel Gabriel’s announcement to Mary about the birth of Jesus.

Catholic.title: Palm Sunday
Catholic.description: Marks Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem at the start of Holy Week.

Catholic.title: Holy Week Begins
Catholic.description: From today Holy Week begins, recalling Jesus’ passion and resurrection.

Catholic.title: Holy Monday
Catholic.description: Continues Holy Week with reflection on Jesus’ teachings and actions.

Catholic.title: Holy Tuesday
Catholic.description: Continues Holy Week with reflection on Jesus’ teachings and warnings.

Catholic.title: Holy Wednesday
Catholic.description: Recalls events leading to the betrayal of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Maundy Thursday
Catholic.description: Commemorates the Last Supper and Jesus’ commandment of love.

Catholic.title: Holy Saturday
Catholic.description: Remembers Christ’s burial and the Church’s waiting before Easter.

Catholic.title: Easter Season Begins
Catholic.description: From today the Easter season begins, celebrating the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Divine Mercy Sunday
Catholic.description: Highlights the mercy of God revealed through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

Catholic.title: Second Sunday of Easter
Catholic.description: Continues the celebration of the resurrection during the Easter season.

Catholic.description: Continues the celebration of the resurrection during the Easter season.

Catholic.description: Continues the celebration of the resurrection during the Easter season.

Catholic.description: Continues the celebration of the resurrection during the Easter season.

Catholic.description: From today the Easter season concludes with Pentecost in many churches.

Catholic.description: Honors the Eucharist and the real presence of Christ in the sacrament.

Catholic.title: Most Holy Trinity
Catholic.description: Celebrates the mystery of one God in three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

Catholic.description: A Sunday in Ordinary Time focusing on Christian discipleship and daily faith.

Orthodox.description: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ according to the Julian calendar tradition.

Orthodox.title: Theophany
Orthodox.description: Celebrates the baptism of Jesus Christ in the Jordan River.

Orthodox.title: Three Holy Hierarchs
Orthodox.description: Honors Saints Basil the Great, Gregory the Theologian, and John Chrysostom.

Orthodox.title: Meeting of the Lord
Orthodox.description: Commemorates Christ being presented in the Temple and encountered by Simeon and Anna.

Orthodox.title: Great Lent Begins
Orthodox.description: From today Great Lent begins, a season of fasting and repentance in preparation for Pascha.

Orthodox.title: Sunday of Saint Gregory Palamas
Orthodox.description: Emphasizes prayer and the experience of God’s grace during Great Lent.

Orthodox.title: Lazarus Saturday
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the raising of Lazarus and anticipates Christ’s victory over death.

Orthodox.title: Palm Sunday
Orthodox.description: Marks Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem at the start of Holy Week.

Orthodox.title: Great and Holy Monday
Orthodox.description: Begins Orthodox Holy Week with themes of watchfulness and repentance.

Orthodox.title: Annunciation
Orthodox.description: Marks the announcement of the angel Gabriel to Mary about the birth of Jesus.

Orthodox.title: Great and Holy Tuesday
Orthodox.description: Continues Holy Week with calls to readiness and faithful living.

Orthodox.title: Great and Holy Wednesday
Orthodox.description: Recalls repentance and the betrayal of Christ as Holy Week continues.

Orthodox.title: Great and Holy Thursday
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the Mystical Supper and the beginning of the Passion.

Orthodox.title: Great and Holy Friday
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ.

Orthodox.title: Great Lent Ends
Orthodox.description: From today Great Lent concludes on the eve of Pascha after Holy Saturday.

Orthodox.title: Great and Holy Saturday
Orthodox.description: Remembers Christ’s rest in the tomb and his victory over death.

Orthodox.title: Thomas Sunday
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the apostle Thomas and the affirmation of Christ’s resurrection.

Orthodox.title: Sunday of the Myrrh-bearing Women
Orthodox.description: Honors the women who discovered the empty tomb of Christ.

Orthodox.title: Sunday of the Paralytic
Orthodox.description: Recalls the healing of the paralytic by Jesus Christ.

Orthodox.title: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
Orthodox.description: Remembers Christ’s encounter with the Samaritan woman at the well.

Orthodox.title: Sunday of the Blind Man
Orthodox.description: Recalls the healing of the man born blind by Jesus Christ.

Orthodox.title: Ascension of the Lord
Orthodox.description: Marks Christ’s ascension into heaven forty days after Pascha.

Orthodox.title: All Saints Sunday
Orthodox.description: Honors all saints on the first Sunday after Pentecost in the Orthodox tradition.

Orthodox.title: Apostles’ Fast Begins
Orthodox.description: From today the Apostles’ Fast begins, preparing for the feast of Saints Peter and Paul.

Orthodox.title: Sunday of All Saints of the Russian Land
Orthodox.description: Honors saints particularly venerated in the Russian Orthodox tradition.

Orthodox.title: Apostles’ Fast Ends
Orthodox.description: From today the Apostles’ Fast concludes on the eve of the feast of Saints Peter and Paul.

Orthodox.title: Saints Peter and Paul
Orthodox.description: Honors the apostles Peter and Paul, pillars of the Orthodox Church.

Orthodox.title: Dormition Fast Begins
Orthodox.description: From today the Dormition Fast begins, preparing for the Dormition feast.

Orthodox.title: Transfiguration
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the transfiguration of Jesus Christ on Mount Tabor.

Orthodox.title: Dormition Fast Ends
Orthodox.description: From today the Dormition Fast concludes on the eve of the Dormition feast.

Orthodox.title: Dormition of the Theotokos
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the falling asleep of the Mother of God.

Orthodox.title: Beheading of Saint John the Baptist
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the martyrdom of Saint John the Baptist and is traditionally a strict fast day.

Orthodox.title: Nativity of the Theotokos
Orthodox.description: Celebrates the birth of the Mother of God.

Orthodox.title: Exaltation of the Holy Cross
Orthodox.description: Commemorates the exaltation of the Cross and its victory in Christian life.

Orthodox.title: Protection of the Theotokos
Orthodox.description: Celebrates the protection and intercession of the Mother of God.

Orthodox.title: Nativity Fast Begins
Orthodox.description: From today the Nativity Fast begins, a period of prayer and preparation for Christmas.

Orthodox.title: Entrance of the Theotokos into the Temple
Orthodox.description: Remembers the dedication of Mary to God in the Temple.

Baptist.title: Palm Sunday
Baptist.description: Marks Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem at the start of Holy Week.

Baptist.title: Holy Thursday
Baptist.description: Many churches commemorate the Last Supper and Jesus’ commandment of love.

Baptist.title: Good Friday
Baptist.description: Commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ.

Baptist.title: Holy Saturday
Baptist.description: Remembers Christ’s burial and the Church’s waiting before Easter.

Baptist.title: Easter Sunday
Baptist.description: Celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ, the central event of the Christian faith.

Baptist.title: Ascension
Baptist.description: Many churches remember Jesus’ ascension into heaven after the resurrection.

Baptist.title: Pentecost
Baptist.description: Commemorates the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the Church.

Baptist.title: Advent Begins
Baptist.description: From today the season of Advent begins, a time of preparation for Christmas.

Baptist.title: Christmas Eve
Baptist.description: Many churches hold services on the evening before Christmas to celebrate Christ’s birth.

Baptist.title: Christmas Day
Baptist.description: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ.

Nondenominational.description: Marks Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem at the start of Holy Week.

Nondenominational.title: Holy Thursday
Nondenominational.description: Many churches commemorate the Last Supper and Jesus’ commandment of love.

Nondenominational.title: Good Friday
Nondenominational.description: Commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ.

Nondenominational.description: Remembers Christ’s burial and the Church’s waiting before Easter.

Nondenominational.description: Celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ, the central event of the Christian faith.

Nondenominational.description: Many churches remember Jesus’ ascension into heaven after the resurrection.

Nondenominational.title: Pentecost
Nondenominational.description: Commemorates the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the Church.

Nondenominational.title: Advent Begins
Nondenominational.description: From today the season of Advent begins, a time of preparation for Christmas.

Nondenominational.title: Christmas Eve
Nondenominational.description: Many churches hold services on the evening before Christmas to celebrate Christ’s birth.

Nondenominational.title: Christmas Day
Nondenominational.description: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ.

Pentecostal.description: Marks Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem at the start of Holy Week.

Pentecostal.title: Holy Thursday
Pentecostal.description: Many churches commemorate the Last Supper and Jesus’ commandment of love.

Pentecostal.title: Good Friday
Pentecostal.description: Commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ.

Pentecostal.title: Holy Saturday
Pentecostal.description: Remembers Christ’s burial and the Church’s waiting before Easter.

Pentecostal.description: Celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ, the central event of the Christian faith.

Pentecostal.description: Many churches remember Jesus’ ascension into heaven after the resurrection.

Pentecostal.title: Pentecost
Pentecostal.description: Commemorates the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the Church.

Pentecostal.title: Advent Begins
Pentecostal.description: From today the season of Advent begins, a time of preparation for Christmas.

Pentecostal.title: Christmas Eve
Pentecostal.description: Many churches hold services on the evening before Christmas to celebrate Christ’s birth.

Pentecostal.title: Christmas Day
Pentecostal.description: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ.

Evangelical.title: Palm Sunday
Evangelical.description: Marks Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem at the start of Holy Week.

Evangelical.title: Holy Thursday
Evangelical.description: Many churches commemorate the Last Supper and Jesus’ commandment of love.

Evangelical.title: Good Friday
Evangelical.description: Commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ.

Evangelical.title: Holy Saturday
Evangelical.description: Remembers Christ’s burial and the Church’s waiting before Easter.

Evangelical.title: Easter Sunday
Evangelical.description: Celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ, the central event of the Christian faith.

Evangelical.title: Ascension
Evangelical.description: Many churches remember Jesus’ ascension into heaven after the resurrection.

Evangelical.title: Pentecost
Evangelical.description: Commemorates the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the Church.

Evangelical.title: Advent Begins
Evangelical.description: From today the season of Advent begins, a time of preparation for Christmas.

Evangelical.title: Christmas Eve
Evangelical.description: Many churches hold services on the evening before Christmas to celebrate Christ’s birth.

Evangelical.title: Christmas Day
Evangelical.description: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ.

Adventist.title: Good Friday
Adventist.description: Commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ.

Adventist.title: Resurrection of Christ (Easter)
Adventist.description: Many Adventist communities reflect on the resurrection of Jesus Christ during this period.

Adventist.title: Pentecost
Adventist.description: Commemorates the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the Church.

Adventist.title: Advent Begins
Adventist.description: From today the season of Advent begins, a time of preparation for Christmas.

Adventist.title: Christmas Day
Adventist.description: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ.
